input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your MAUI page theme-aware:

## XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` listens to the OS theme and swaps values automatically — no event handling needed.

## C# alternative — `SetAppTheme`

For code-behind or dynamic scenarios, use the extension method on any `BindableObject`:

```csharp
// In your page constructor or OnAppearing:
this.SetAppTheme(
    ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")); // dark

myLabel.SetAppTheme(
    Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

myButton.SetAppTheme(
    Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

## Key points

| Concern | Approach |
|---|---|
| **Static XAML values** | `{AppThemeBinding Light=…, Dark=…}` |
| **Code-behind** | `element.SetAppTheme(property, light, dark)` |
| **Resources/styles** | Use `AppThemeBinding` inside a `Style` or `ResourceDictionary` for reuse |
| **Detection** | `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` returns `AppTheme.Light` or `AppTheme.Dark` |
| **Change event** | `Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged` fires on OS toggle |

Both approaches react to OS theme changes at runtime with no extra wiring.
